hero.tagPill: BMW X7 • 2018–Present • 1 Generation (G07) • B58, B57, N63, S68, S63
hero.h1: X7 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW X7 engine across the G07 generation — from the B58 straight-six and B57 diesel to the S68 twin-turbo V8 in the M60i and ALPINA XB7. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's flagship luxury SUV. Backed by real UK X7 engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ✅ Usually Worth Replacing — a B58 recon at £3,800–£6,000 against a £25,000–£45,000 used X7 is 8–24% — ✅ safe. An S68 recon at £9,000–£15,000+ against a £70,000–£100,000+ M60i is 9–21% — ✅ safe. The X7's values are strong enough to justify engine work.
★★★★☆ — the B58 and B57 are the modern gold standard; the S68 is proving robust; the N63 in pre-LCI cars carries the hot-vee caveats
B58B30 (xDrive40i) — widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines
N63B44D (pre-LCI M50i) — oil consumption, valve stem seals, timing chain issues
2023+ G07 LCI xDrive40i with B58 — the most dependable X7 powertrain ever made, with 48V mild-hybrid assistance
G07 LCI X7 M60i with S68 — 523 hp, 48V mild-hybrid, the most powerful standard X7
B58 coolant system / N63 oil consumption — shared-platform data [BMW-VERIFIED]
👑 The Flagship SUV — BMW's largest, most luxurious, and most expensive SUV

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The X7 is BMW's flagship SUV — one generation, two distinct eras, and the B58 straight-six is the most dependable engine ever fitted to the badge. The LCI brought the S68 V8 and 48V mild-hybrid assistance, but the B58 remains the gold standard. Buy the B58 unless you have the budget for V8 bills — and on any pre-LCI N63, budget for oil consumption checks before you buy.

Our Verdict: BMW's flagship SUV — the largest, most luxurious, and most expensive BMW ever built. The B58 xDrive40i is the dependable choice; the S68 M60i is the performance pick. The LCI (2023+) brought 48V mild-hybrid assistance and the S68 V8. Buy the B58 unless you have the budget for V8 bills.

rows.metric: The Badge Ambiguity — "M50i" vs "M60i" Means Different Things
rows.value: Correction applied (internal): The "M50i" badge (pre-LCI) uses the N63B44D V8 — the same hot-vee V8 that carries oil consumption and valve stem seal caveats from earlier generations. The "M60i" badge (LCI) uses the S68B44A V8 — a significantly revised engine with 48V mild-hybrid assistance, producing 523 hp. The S68 is proving more robust than the N63. The "ALPINA XB7" uses a tuned version of the S68 producing up to 631 hp.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X7 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW X7 — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: B58B30 and B57D30 ratings: 90% confidence — high-volume engines with deep specialist rebuild history and clear failure patterns from the wider BMW range. N63B44D rating: 85% confidence — the N63's hot-vee V8 issues are well-documented across the 5/6/7/X5/X6 ranges. S68B44A rating: Provisional — the G07 LCI is too new for volume data; ratings reflect early patterns and shared-architecture evidence from the S63. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The X7 M50i (pre-LCI) uses the N63B44D engine. This is the same hot-vee V8 that carries oil consumption and valve stem seal caveats from earlier generations (N63B44, N63B44B). Correction applied: The X7 M60i (LCI) uses the S68B44A engine — a significantly revised V8 with 48V mild-hybrid assistance, producing 523 hp. This is not the same as the N63. Correction applied: The ALPINA XB7 uses a tuned version of the S68 producing 631 hp — the most powerful X7 variant. Correction applied: The LCI (2023+) brought 48V mild-hybrid assistance to all petrol models, including the B58 xDrive40i.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the X7 range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The B58's known weak point — coolant expansion tank leaks, water pump failures, and thermostat housing cracks. Affects all B58-powered X7 (2018+). £400–£800 repair if caught early.
The B58 engine can experience idle roughness and vibration, particularly when the air conditioning is off. This is a recognised characteristic of the B58 in the X7.
The N63 V8 suffers from excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles), valve stem seal wear, and timing chain stretch. Affects pre-LCI M50i (2019–2022). £7,000–£10,000+ recon.

The N63's hot-vee design causes cooling system failures (water pump, expansion tank) and turbocharger issues from heat soak. Affects pre-LCI M50i. Cooling repairs: £500–£1,500; turbo repairs: £3,000–£6,000.
The B57 diesel's primary weak points — DPF clogging on urban-driven cars and EGR cooler issues. Affects all B57-powered X7 (2018+). Well-documented, repairable.
Not an engine failure — but a common non-engine X7 enquiry. The G07's air suspension can develop leaks; the transfer case can wear. Check on any high-mileage G07.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW X7 Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the flagship SUV is entering used market [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — the LCI is the most popular [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — more cars entering warranty-exit age

insights: Interesting insight: The X7 generates a different kind of enquiry than the X5 — buyers are often luxury SUV buyers doing due diligence on specific known issues (B58 coolant, N63 oil consumption) rather than families facing unexpected failures. The X7 buyer is often a high-net-worth individual making a considered purchase, making the engine choice a critical decision point. The B58 xDrive40i is the safe choice; the V8s are for the committed. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The pre-LCI X7 is now entering affordable territory — £25,000–£35,000 for a used example. But the N63 V8 questions mean buyers need to budget accordingly: a £7,000–£10,000+ recon on a £25,000 car is a significant consideration. The B58 xDrive40i is the robust alternative — it's the most dependable X7 powertrain ever made. (Editorial synthesis.)

The LCI X7 with the B58 or S68 is the generation to buy — the B58 is bulletproof, the S68 is proving robust, and the 48V mild-hybrid assistance adds refinement and efficiency. The ALPINA XB7 is the ultimate X7 — 631 hp and a truly bespoke luxury experience. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW X7 Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one X7 based on this page, buy a 2023+ LCI with the B58 xDrive40i — it's the most dependable X7 ever made, the B58 is bulletproof, and the 48V mild-hybrid adds refinement. If the budget only stretches to a pre-LCI car, buy the B58 — the N63 V8 in the M50i is the trap, not the bargain. And on any N63, budget for oil consumption checks before you buy — it's £7,000–£10,000+ well spent.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X7 Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW X7 engine typically takes 12–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The X7 rule — flagship, safe: A B58 recon at £3,800–£6,000 against a £25,000–£35,000 pre-LCI X7 sits at 11–24% — ✅ safe. Against a £45,000–£55,000 LCI X7, the same recon is 7–13% — ✅ safe. An S68 recon at £9,000–£15,000+ against a £80,000–£100,000+ M60i is 9–19% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe. The X7's values are strong enough to justify engine work — the B58 xDrive40i is the one to buy; the pre-LCI N63 M50i is the one to avoid without oil consumption history.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW X7 Engine Range Evolved — 2018 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW launched the X7 as the flagship SUV — the largest, most luxurious, and most expensive BMW ever built. The B58 straight-six and B57 diesel were the dependable choices; the N63 V8 carried the hot-vee caveats.
eras.worthKnowing: The X7 is the BMW for buyers who need seven seats and ultimate luxury. The B58 xDrive40i is the sweet spot — 340 bhp, 30 mpg, and bulletproof reliability.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The LCI brought 48V mild-hybrid assistance across the range and replaced the N63 with the S68 V8. The B58 received an upgrade to 375 bhp with 48V MHEV. The ALPINA XB7 was also updated with the S68.
eras.worthKnowing: The S68 is a significant improvement over the N63 — 523 bhp, 48V mild-hybrid, and 0-62 mph in 4.7 seconds. The X7 LCI is the generation to buy — the B58 is bulletproof, the S68 is proving robust, and the ALPINA XB7 is the ultimate X7.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW X7?

profiles.verdict: The X7 is the ultimate family SUV — seven seats, 750 litres of boot space (with seats down), and the B57 diesel delivers 35+ mpg. The B58 is the petrol pick; the B57 is the sensible diesel. The LCI with 48V MHEV is even more refined.
The X7 is a large SUV — it's not a city car. But the B58 xDrive40i with 48V MHEV returns 30+ mpg and the CLAR platform is supremely comfortable. The B57 diesel is the economy pick.

A cheap pre-LCI N63 M50i is not a cheap X7 — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £30,000 pre-LCI B58 xDrive40i is the bargain; a £25,000 M50i without oil consumption history is the trap.

The X7 M60i with the S68 is a genuine performance SUV — 523 hp, 48V mild-hybrid, and 0-62 mph in 4.7 seconds. The ALPINA XB7 is the ultimate X7 — 631 hp and a bespoke luxury experience. Both are the flagship X7s.
The G07 B58 xDrive40i is the modern keeper — the B58 will outlast the car around it, and the X7's values are holding strong. The LCI B58 with 48V MHEV is the one to buy for the long term.

items.question: How much does a BMW X7 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW X7 engine typically costs £3,800–£9,000+ supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the B58 at £3,800–£6,000 to the N63 at £7,000–£10,000+. The S63/S68 costs £9,000–£15,000+. Fitting adds £650–£2,100. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

items.question: Which BMW X7 engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The B58B30 straight-six in the xDrive40i — widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. The B57D30 diesel is also excellent. The S68B44A in the LCI M60i is proving robust.

items.question: Which BMW X7 engine should I avoid?
items.answer: No engine outright — but a pre-LCI N63B44D M50i without documented oil consumption history, valve stem seal replacement, or timing chain history is the one purchase we consistently advise against. A B58 or LCI S68 has no such caveat.

items.question: How do I know if my X7 engine needs replacing?
items.answer: By engine: coolant loss (B58 cooling system), blue smoke on startup (N63 valve stem seals), rattling on cold start (N63 timing chain), DPF clogging (B57 diesel). On the pre-LCI N63, any excessive oil consumption is a warning sign. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £3,800–£10,000+ engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW X7 engine?
items.answer: Yes — the X7's values are strong enough to justify engine work. Against a £25,000–£35,000 pre-LCI X7, a £3,800–£6,000 B58 recon is 11–24% — ✅ safe. Against a £45,000–£55,000 LCI X7, the same recon is 7–13% — ✅ safe. Against a £80,000–£100,000+ M60i, a £9,000–£15,000+ S68 recon is 9–19% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe. Use our calculator for your car.

items.question: Which BMW X7 should I buy?
items.answer: 2023+ LCI with the B58 xDrive40i — 375 bhp, 48V mild-hybrid, the most dependable X7 ever made, and the values are holding strong (£45,000–£55,000). For performance, the LCI M60i with the S68 — 523 hp and 0-62 mph in 4.7 seconds. For the budget option, a pre-LCI B58 xDrive40i — the B58 is bulletproof and the values are sensible (£25,000–£35,000). Avoid pre-LCI N63 M50i without documented oil consumption history.

items.question: How do I find my BMW X7 engine code?
items.answer: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation and variant (G07 xDrive40i, G07 M50i, G07 M60i) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

items.question: BMW X7 vs X5 — which is more reliable?
items.answer: Mechanically they share engines across similar eras — the B58/B57/N63/S63/S68 story is similar. The X7 is larger and heavier, so it works the powertrain harder; the X5 is smaller and lighter. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The X5 offers more value; the X7 offers more space and prestige.
